pattern: Sweeping the closing hour left to right updates the penalty incrementally, since opening one more hour adds a miss for a quiet hour or removes a miss for a busy one.
hints: Compare consecutive closing times instead of recomputing each from scratch: moving the close one hour later only changes the penalty by the status of that single hour.
If the hour you newly include had a customer, the penalty drops by one; if it was empty, the penalty rises by one.
Start with the penalty for closing immediately (all customers missed), then scan hours tracking the running penalty and remember the earliest minimum.

edge_inputs.description: all busy hours close at the end

stress.note: linear reference handles thousands of hours quickly
